title: Silent partial decode: empty if-block when rgb_write_row != ah
description: After the tile decode loop, if `rgb_write_row != ah` (row count mismatch), the code enters an empty `if` block and silently returns a partially-decoded image with zero-filled rows. No error is returned, no log is emitted, and the caller receives a truncated buffer as if it were a full decode.
fix: Replace empty block with `return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("DNG tile decode incomplete: {} of {} rows", rgb_write_row, ah))` or at minimum fill remaining rows and log a warning.

title: Missing JxlDecoderCloseInput in decode_progressive_frames_borrowed
description: `decode_progressive_frames_borrowed` calls `JxlDecoderSetInput` but never calls `JxlDecoderCloseInput` before the event loop. The full-decode path (`run_full_into`) correctly calls `CloseInput`. Without it, libjxl may return `NeedMoreInput` instead of completing, causing the function to silently break out of the loop and return `None` from the frame collector, losing the partial result with no error propagated.
snippet: JxlDecoderSetInput(dec, ptr, len); // CloseInput never called
fix: Call `JxlDecoderCloseInput(dec)` immediately after `JxlDecoderSetInput` (mirrors the pattern in `run_full_into` at line 478).

title: Null thread runner stored silently in Decoder::new when JxlThreadParallelRunnerCreate fails
description: When `opts.parallel` is true and `JxlThreadParallelRunnerCreate` returns a null pointer, the null is stored as the runner and `JxlDecoderSetParallelRunner` is called with it. libjxl's behaviour with a null runner is undefined; in practice it silently falls back to single-threaded operation, but the caller received no indication that parallel mode was not engaged.
snippet:
let runner = unsafe { JxlThreadParallelRunnerCreate(...) };
// no null check — stored directly
fix: Check `runner.is_null()` and either return `Err(...)` or fall back to scalar mode with a log message before calling `JxlDecoderSetParallelRunner`.

title: decode_jxtc_region silently zeros failed tile regions
description: In the JXTC tile decode loop, `dec_slot.as_mut()?` inside the closure returns `None` on tile decode failure (the `?` propagates to the closure return, not the outer function). Failed tiles leave their region in `dest` as zero bytes (black). The caller receives a partially-black composite image with no indication of which tiles failed or how many.
fix: Collect per-tile errors explicitly. Either return `Err` if any tile fails (fail-fast) or log each failure and track a `failed_tiles` counter, returning it to the caller alongside the composite buffer.

title: walk_inner has no IFD entry count cap (unlike cr2.rs which caps at 512)
description: `walk_inner` iterates up to the full TIFF IFD entry count (u16: 0..65535) without any cap. `cr2.rs` explicitly caps at 512. A corrupt or malicious DNG with a large IFD count causes O(n) parse time per IFD, multiplied by recursive sub-IFD chains, allowing a DoS via crafted input.
fix: Add `if entry_count > 512 { return Err(...) }` guard at the top of `walk_inner`, matching the `cr2.rs` pattern.

title: box_downscale_rgba8 silently returns all-zeros dst on size mismatch
description: `box_downscale_rgba8` checks `src.len() < src_len || dst.len() < dst_len` and returns early (plain `return`, not `Err`) leaving `dst` as all zeros. The caller `encode_rgba8_pyramid` then proceeds to encode the zero-filled buffer as a valid pyramid level, producing a silent black sidecar image.
snippet: if src.len() < src_len || dst.len() < dst_len { return; }
fix: Change the function signature to return `Result<(), anyhow::Error>` and propagate an error, or `assert!` the precondition (callers control the sizes and this should never happen in correct usage).

title: Output buffer grow-loop in encode_inner has no maximum size guard
description: The encode output buffer doubles on each `NeedMoreOutput` from libjxl with no upper bound. A misbehaving encoder (e.g. libjxl bug, corrupt state) could loop indefinitely, doubling allocation until OOM. No iteration count limit or size cap exists.
fix: Add a cap: `if buf.len() > MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES { return Err(...) }` before the resize, where `MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES` is a generous constant (e.g. `input_pixels * 4 * 2` or `256 MB`).

title: chrono_now() produces invalid month values (up to 13) due to naive doy/30 math
description: `chrono_now` computes month as `doy / 30 + 1`. For `doy >= 360` (e.g. Dec 26-31), `doy/30 = 12`, so `month = 13`. This produces file names like `2026-13-01T...` which are invalid ISO 8601 timestamps and will cause issues with any tooling that parses the filename.
fix: Use a cumulative month-day table (or the `chrono` crate) to correctly compute month/day-of-month from day-of-year, including leap year handling.

title: ct6_progressive_dc2_truncated_stream test is a complete stub (assert!(true))
description: The test `ct6_progressive_dc2_truncated_stream` is named to cover truncated JXL stream handling, which is a critical error path (the decoder must not panic or corrupt state on abrupt stream end). The body is `assert!(true)` — zero coverage. If libjxl changes its truncation behaviour, no test catches it.
fix: Implement the test: encode a real JXL, truncate the byte stream at several offsets, call the decoder, and assert it returns an error (not panics) for each truncation point.

title: No structured error taxonomy — anyhow strings, thiserror enums, and raw strings mixed
description: Error handling uses three patterns inconsistently: `anyhow::anyhow!("...")` (most of the codebase), `thiserror`-derived enums (jxl_casadecoder.rs `DecodeError`), and bare `String` errors (some older paths). Callers cannot match on error kinds without string parsing. Consistent structured error types would enable programmatic error handling (e.g. distinguishing `BudgetExceeded` from `CorruptInput` from `IoError`).
fix: Define a crate-level `RawPipelineError` enum with `thiserror`; migrate `anyhow` usages at public boundaries to typed variants. Internal helpers may keep `anyhow` for convenience.

title: No timeout pattern on filesystem and libjxl calls
description: Functions that read files (`std::fs::read` in examples and tests), call libjxl's encode/decode event loop, or perform DNG tile decompression have no timeout mechanism. A hung filesystem mount or a libjxl encode that loops indefinitely (see errors-7) blocks the calling thread forever. This is expected for a library crate, but the absence of any cancellation token or deadline parameter means integrators must wrap everything in a thread with an external watchdog.
fix: For the encode/decode loops, a cancellation-flag approach (`AtomicBool`) passed into the loop is the minimal viable pattern. Document the absence of timeouts in public API docs as a known limitation.
